items.headline: Mexican Heritage Plaza receives California state arts grant
items.summary: The School of Arts and Culture at Mexican Heritage Plaza secured funding from the California Natural Resources Agency. This funding will support cultural programming at the South Bay institution.

items.headline: Active Transportation Plan Update: Phase 2 Results and Revised Scoring
items.summary: The City Council received an update on Cupertino's Active Transportation Plan development, including Phase 2 findings and changes to how projects will be scored and prioritized. This plan will guide future bike lanes, pedestrian paths, and transit improvements across the city.

items.headline: Council Vacancy Process Changes Under Review
items.summary: The City Council is considering changes to how council vacancies are filled, including a potential reduction in the process requirements. This could affect how quickly residents see representation on the council when seats become open.

items.headline: Major elevator upgrade project at Ridder Park commercial complex
items.summary: A $1.1 million elevator modernization project is underway at 1290 Ridder Park Dr, suggesting significant investment in maintaining or improving a major commercial building. This scale of infrastructure upgrade typically indicates either a large office/mixed-use complex or preparation for increased occupancy.

items.headline: Legal Case: Garden Gate Coalition Challenges City on Mary Avenue Safety
items.summary: The City Council met in closed session regarding ongoing litigation with the Garden Gate Coalition over Mary Avenue safety concerns. This case suggests a contested dispute between residents and the city over traffic or pedestrian safety measures on that street.

items.headline: Commercial/industrial development approved on West San Carlos
items.summary: A $696,570 commercial or industrial project is moving forward at 1300 W San Carlos St. This signals continued commercial activity along the West San Carlos corridor, a key industrial and business area in San José.

items.headline: Gas Utility Rates May Change for Fiscal Year 2027
items.summary: The City Council is considering amendments to gas rate schedules for residential and commercial customers starting in fiscal year 2027. The specific rate changes will affect household utility bills and business operating costs.

items.headline: City Launches Business Amnesty Program and Retail Incentives
items.summary: Cupertino announced a Business License Amnesty Program targeting shopping centers and rolled out new economic development initiatives including retail incentives, a summer shop-local campaign, and public-private event sponsorships. These programs aim to support small businesses and local commerce in the city.
